description:
When both operands of an arithmetic op are literal-narrow (e.g. 1 + 1, or 2 * n where n is literal 3), the result Descr is widened to the base type (int, float) rather than the computed literal value.

Affected fixtures: hello, macro_inc, spec_ok, vr1_int_arith, vr3_4_typed_capture, vr4_2_native_call, vr5b_typed_print, add1

Example: add1(3) should produce a spec where the return is literally 4, but it shows int.

Fix: in ir_typer, when both operand Descrs are singleton literal sets of the same numeric type, evaluate the op at type-inference time and return the literal result Descr.
title: ir_typer: arithmetic on literal operands not constant-folded to literal result
